title: LoL Esports |
content:
Following a riveting MSI, the League of Legends Championship Pacific (LCP) returns on 25 July for its third and final Split! The stakes are higher than ever as teams battle to become the 2026 LCP champion while also vying for Worlds qualification.

In partnership with VNGGames, Carry International, PlayBrain, and Invate, the upcoming split will also introduce the Swiss Stage for the first time in LCP to keep the competition fresh, and making every win and loss even more important on the road to Finals Weekend Taipei.

LCP Split 3 starts from 25 July and runs till 30 August. Each matchday will start at4pm GMT+7 // 5pm GMT+8 // 6pm GMT+9 // 8pm GMT+11.

The full LCP schedule can be found atlolesports.comwith routine updates on our socials.

This split introduces the Swiss Stage format to the Regular Season. Here’s a refresher on how it works.

In the first round, the top 4 teams from Split 2 Knockout Stage have been drawn randomly against the bottom 4 teams. First week’s matchups are as follows:

title: MSI 2026 Grand Final falls short of T1 vs G2 viewership peak
summary: MSI 2026 Grand Final falls short of T1 vs G2 viewership peak  Esports Charts
content:
LIVE Viewership Updates & Audience Trends

Hanwha Life EsportsdefeatedBilibili Gaming(3:2) to win the2026 Mid-Season Invitational, claiming the organization’s first MSI title in its debut appearance at the tournament. The victory also kept the trophy in South Korea for a third consecutive season, following Gen.G’s back-to-back championships in 2024 and 2025.

The grand final reached 2.27 million Peak Viewers (excluding Chinese livestreaming platforms), falling short of the 2.32 million recorded duringT1’s match againstG2 Esportsearlier in the bracket. This made MSI 2026 the first edition since 2023 in which the grand final was not the tournament’s most-watched match.

MSI 2026 recorded a noticeable year-over-year decline compared with the record-setting 2025 edition. Total Hours Watched fell by more than 17%, while Peak Viewers dropped by over 32%. Part of that decline can be explained by the tournament’s shorter airtime, with this year’s event running roughly eight hours less than in 2025.

T1’s relatively early elimination was another major factor: the team remains the biggest viewership driver not only at MSI, but across the wider League of Legends esports ecosystem, and interest naturally weakened once it exited the competition.

Average Viewers proved considerably more resilient, declining by just 7.8% year over year. This suggests that, despite the loss of its strongest audience draw and a shorter overall broadcast schedule, MSI 2026 still maintained a solid underlying level of interest throughout the event. The downturn was therefore meaningful, but less severe on a per-minute basis than the headline Hours Watched and peak figures might suggest.

title: 'LCK Legends Match' to be Held at LoL PARK on August 4
summary: 'LCK Legends Match' to be Held at LoL PARK on August 4  Inven Global
content:
A stage is being set for a journey back in time to the early days of domestic League of Legends esports, an era often remembered as the age of romance.

Riot Games, the organizer of the League of Legends Champions Korea (LCK), announced on the 14th that it will host the LCK Legends Match at LoL PARK in Jongno-gu, Seoul, on August 4.

Riot Games previously held an event match featuring League of Legends Classic (LoL Classic) at the 2026 Mid-Season Invitational (MSI) finals, which took place at the Daejeon Convention Center (DCC) in Yuseong-gu, Daejeon. The match electrified the crowd with appearances by former professional players from Korea and abroad, including 'Ambition' Kang Chan-yong, 'PawN' Heo Won-seok, and 'Captain Jack' Kang Hyung-woo.

Immediately following the LoL Classic event match, Riot Games unveiled information regarding regional special matches, and the LCK released a teaser video for the LCK Legends Match. The video outlines plans to summon the raw, unfiltered gameplay that frequently defined the so-called age of romance, when players competed purely on instinct.

K/DA and Taric - Coa, Haeun, Yeovlynn, Rakang, Bong

Creative use of ZEPETO - Abigelic Cosplay Spot

title: League of Legends Classic is arriving much sooner than expected
summary: League of Legends Classic is arriving much sooner than expected  Polygon.com
content:
We'll be able to play this "greatest hits" version of League of Legends by the end of the month

Riot Games has fully unveiledLeague of Legends Classicafter teasing it a couple of weeks ago. It's coming out sooner than expected. It's being added to the game for free on July 29 as part of Patch 26.15.

These new details were revealed during the MSI 2026 Finals for LoL Esports, where anexhibition matchfor the new mode was also held. An FAQ onLeague of Legends Classic'sofficial websiteconfirms it willbe integrated right into the Riot Client players already use forLeague of Legendsand is more of a melting pot of classic features than a complete reset tothe 1.0 version of the game.

Executive Producer Paul Bellezza revealed more aboutLeague of Legends Classicduring a brief interview at the MSI Finals. He explained that the idea first emerged during a "Thunderdome" hackathon at Riot Games, when a group of developers built a prototype based onSeason 3 (2012-2013)of the game.

He also clarified that it would be a "greatest hits ofLeague" from around Season 3 rather than a completely faithful throwback. It'll launch with not just the original 40 champions but also 20 additional "classic" ones.Classicwill bring back the old way of selecting champions and runes as well as the offense, defense, and utility skills trees for the mastery system.

League of Legends Classicis part of a wave of throwback modes or re-releases of earlier versions of long-running live-service games.World of Warcraft Classicmost famously kicked off this trend, but we've also seen the likes ofFortnite OG,EverQuest Legends, andOverwatch: Classicpop up in the years since. It's fascinating that these live-service games have been running for so long that they have older versions players are nostalgic for and excited to revisit.

title: Mid-Season Invitational | Play-Ins
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'Mid-Season Invitational | Play-Ins'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

In Europe's LEC, the atmosphere is electric as teams prepare to integrate these updates into their drafts. G2 Esports and Fnatic have historically led the charge in innovative pocket picks and creative map rotations. This new development could provide the perfect opportunity for rising rosters to challenge the established order and secure crucial championship points for the upcoming international events.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'Mid-Season Invitational | Play-Ins  LoL Esports' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.
